title: The Cyclic (2291,230,23) Difference-Set Case
lean_gate_reason: Lean is a secondary archival seal here, not the gating publication step. The packet is already human-ready once the exact-row proof artifacts and compact mod-29 elimination certificate are preserved.
micro_paper_assessment: Human-ready exact residual-row packet: a verified nonexistence theorem for one Baumert-Gordon survivor, bounded no-rediscovery audit, and preserved proof certificate. Lean is optional later sealing rather than a publication gate.
family_affinity: The proof candidate sits squarely inside the Baumert-Gordon residual cyclic lane: one full multiplier, one small quotient elimination, and one complementary quotient moment contradiction.
generalization_signal: The multiplier-normalize-compress-transfer template is reusable, but the final contradiction here depends on tuple-specific small second moment on the complementary quotient.
candidate_theorem_slice: No cyclic (2291,230,23)-difference set exists; a full 23-multiplier forces a unique mod-29 contraction profile (13;4,7,10,10) up to orbit relabeling, which then forces mod-79 zero fiber 29 and an impossible second moment.
strongest_honest_claim: Within the bounded prior-art audit completed on 2026-04-15, the cyclic group C_2291 admits no (2291,230,23)-difference set. A full 23-multiplier and translation normalization reduce the problem to a unique mod-29 contraction profile (13;4,7,10,10), which forces mod-79 zero fiber 29 and contradicts the required second moment. No earlier direct settlement of this exact row surfaced in the bounded audit.
next_action: This packet is HUMAN_READY and has been moved to LEAN_QUEUE for formal sealing; do not block fresh curation/solve work on pending Lean.
